experiment: ADR-098 nucleus with REAL multi-file content (ADR-117)
method: 5-file repo (intervals.js has a real touching-merge bug; 4 plausible distractors of varied relevance) + a real Node test. The variant's REAL contextBuilder ranks/selects files; the real LLM is given the SELECTED FILES' ACTUAL CONTENT + the failing test and must identify the buggy file and fix it from the code (no hardcoded fix, returns JSON {file,content}); the REAL test command is the verdict.
significance: End-to-end real SWE-style loop at micro scale: real contextBuilder surfaces the right file by relevance, real LLM REASONS over real multi-file code to pick AND fix the bug, real test confirms. Beyond ADR-109 (hardcoded fix) — the LLM does the reasoning. ~$0.0005.
honest_scope: Hand-built 5-file repo, one bug, one model call — a nucleus, not a SWE-bench run. Scaling to a real multi-file corpus with per-variant evolution is the remaining ADR-098 build.
